21.correct: The main nitrogenous waste products excreted by the human kidney are urea and uric acid.

wrong.A: Carbon dioxide and oxygen are exchanged by the lungs, not excreted by the kidney as nitrogenous waste.
wrong.C: Amino acids and glucose are reabsorbed, not excreted normally.
wrong.D: Fatty acids and glycerol are products of fat digestion, not kidney waste.

21.fact: Urea is the major nitrogenous waste in humans, formed in the liver from ammonia.

22.correct: Plastic is a common example of non-biodegradable waste, as it does not decompose naturally for a very long time.
22.fact: Plastic can take hundreds of years to break down, causing long-term pollution.

23.correct: Switching from plastic bags to jute bags is an example of 'reducing' the generation of waste (avoiding single-use plastic).

wrong.A: Reusing a paint bucket several times is 'reuse', not 'reduce'.
wrong.D: Throwing aluminium cans in the bin is neither reduce nor recycle.

23.fact: The waste hierarchy is Reduce, Reuse and Recycle (the 3 Rs).

24.correct: The 'Chhatri' (a domed, pavilion-like canopy) is the architectural element used in Rajput cenotaphs and memorials.

wrong.A: The garbhagriha is the inner sanctum of a temple.
wrong.D: A gopuram is a South Indian temple gateway tower.

24.fact: Chhatris are elevated, dome-shaped pavilions widely used in Rajput and Mughal architecture.

25.correct: Based on function, 'vascular tissue' is NOT a type of animal tissue - vascular tissue (xylem/phloem) is found in plants.

wrong.A: Epithelial tissue is an animal tissue.
wrong.B: Connective tissue is an animal tissue.
wrong.C: Muscular tissue is an animal tissue.

25.fact: The four basic animal tissues are epithelial, connective, muscular and nervous.

26.correct: Animals that live both in water and on land are called amphibians.

wrong.A: Mammals are a class of warm-blooded vertebrates, not defined by living in water and land.

26.fact: Frogs and toads are common examples of amphibians.

27.correct: Decomposers recycle nutrients back into the soil by breaking down dead organic matter.

wrong.A: They are not autotrophs (they cannot make their own food).
wrong.C: They do not produce food for plants directly.
wrong.D: They feed on dead matter, not living beings.

27.fact: Bacteria and fungi are the chief decomposers in an ecosystem.

28.correct: In Odisha, V.K. Pandian retired from active politics in June 2024.

wrong.B: Baijayant Panda is active in politics.
wrong.D: Dharmendra Pradhan is a serving Union Minister.

28.fact: V.K. Pandian was a former bureaucrat who had entered the BJD's political leadership.

29.correct: In August 2024, Daljit Singh Chaudhary was appointed Director-General of the Border Security Force (BSF).

wrong.B: Virendra Bansal is not the appointee.
wrong.C: M.R. Kumar is not the BSF DG.
wrong.D: Nikhil Joshi is not the appointee.

29.fact: The BSF guards India's borders with Pakistan and Bangladesh.

30.correct: The primary function of the villi in the human small intestine is to increase the surface area for the absorption of digested food.

wrong.A: Secreting digestive enzymes is done by glands, not the villi specifically.
wrong.C: Neutralising stomach acid is done by bile/pancreatic juice.
wrong.D: Breaking down food particles is the role of enzymes, not the villi's main function.

30.fact: Millions of tiny finger-like villi greatly increase the intestine's absorptive surface.

31.correct: The Aravalli Green Wall Project does NOT cover Maharashtra; it focuses on Haryana, Rajasthan, Gujarat and Delhi along the Aravalli range.

wrong.A: Haryana is covered by the project.

31.fact: The Aravalli Green Wall aims to create a green belt to check desertification.

32.correct: The SLV-3 rocket, used for India's first satellite launch (Rohini), was a 4-stage rocket.
32.fact: The SLV-3 made India the sixth nation to achieve orbital launch capability (1980).

33.correct: In Chhattisgarh, the 'Faag' (Phag) folk songs are sung to mark the arrival of spring (around Holi).

wrong.C: 'Kavini' is not the spring folk song here.
wrong.D: 'Kolatam' is a stick dance of Andhra/Telangana.

33.fact: Faag songs are associated with the festive Holi season in central India.

34.correct: 'Gajan' folk songs, depicting dialogue among deities, are traditionally associated with West Bengal.
34.fact: The Gajan festival of Bengal is linked to the worship of Shiva and Dharmathakur.

35.correct: The nickname 'Payyoli Express' belongs to the athlete P.T. Usha.

wrong.B: Roshan Mistry is not associated with this nickname.
wrong.C: This option does not name P.T. Usha.
wrong.D: Dutee Chand is a different sprinter.

35.fact: P.T. Usha, from Payyoli in Kerala, narrowly missed an Olympic medal in 1984.

36.correct: Both effects can occur with drug abuse in sport - some drugs slow the heart rate (I) and many cause side effects like vomiting (II) - so 'Both I and II' is correct.

wrong.D: 'Neither' is wrong since both can occur.

36.fact: Doping carries serious health risks and is banned under the WADA Code.

37.correct: The 'Status of Policing in India Report 2025' was published by Common Cause and Lokniti (CSDS).

wrong.B: NITI Aayog did not publish it.
wrong.C: The DARPG did not publish it.
wrong.D: The Ministry of Home Affairs did not publish this report.

37.fact: The report assesses public perceptions and the working conditions of the police.

38.correct: The 'India Social Development Report' is one of the key publications of the Council for Social Development (CSD).

wrong.B: It is not published by the Ministry of Social Justice and Empowerment.
wrong.C: It is not a NITI Aayog publication.
wrong.D: It is not published by the CPCB.

38.fact: The Council for Social Development is a New Delhi-based social-science research institution.

39.correct: Maharishi Valmiki, author of the Ramayana, is known as the 'Adi Kavi' (the first poet) of Indian literature.
39.fact: The Ramayana is regarded as the 'Adi Kavya' (first epic poem) of Sanskrit literature.

40.correct: Dr A.P.J. Abdul Kalam served as the 11th President of India from 2002 to 2007.

wrong.A: Pratibha Patil was President from 2007 to 2012.
wrong.B: Pranab Mukherjee was President from 2012 to 2017.
wrong.D: Shankar Dayal Sharma was President from 1992 to 1997.

40.fact: A.P.J. Abdul Kalam, the 'Missile Man', was a renowned aerospace scientist.
